sourceMovie.title: Rounders
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Rounders works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Wolf of Wall Street
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Rounders clicked for you, The Wolf of Wall Street (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and crime, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Casino
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Rounders clicked for you, Casino (1995) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and crime, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Rounders?
faq.answer: Try The Wolf of Wall Street, Casino, Once Upon a Time in America—each shares DNA with Rounders while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Rounders?
faq.answer: Rounders (1998) is best described as Drama, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Rounders worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
